title: pkcs11: put copies secret into plain Vec<u8>, violates zeroize rule
description: Trigger: any call to put(value). let data = value.as_bytes().to_vec() allocates a plain Vec<u8> not wrapped in Zeroizing. When put returns, that heap region is not zeroed. CLAUDE.md says: never copy inner bytes into a non-Zeroizing buffer inside any backend. Fix: Zeroizing::new(value.as_bytes().to_vec()).
